title: Why Expensive Products Can Be Cheaper Than Subscriptions

sources.title: Paying Not to Go to the Gym

claims.claim: Consumers estimated their monthly subscription spending at 86 dollars, but itemized actual spending averaged 219 dollars, more than 2.5 times higher.
42 percent of consumers have forgotten they were still being charged for a subscription they no longer use.
Gym members on flat monthly fees over 70 dollars attended on average 4.3 times per month, paying more than 17 dollars per expected visit versus a 10 dollar pay-per-visit option.

scenes.narration: Most people guess they spend eighty six dollars a month on subscriptions. The real number is two hundred nineteen.

visual.headline: Subscriptions feel cheap. They are not.

scenes.narration: A big price tag hurts once. A small monthly charge never hurts. So your brain stops counting it.
When researchers made people list every subscription, real spending was two and a half times their estimate. Forty two percent were still paying for something they forgot.

visual.label: actual subscription spending versus what people estimate

scenes.narration: In one famous study, gym members on flat fees paid seventeen dollars a visit, when pay per visit cost ten. Predictable felt cheaper. It was not.

visual.verdict: The safe-feeling option cost 70 percent more.

scenes.narration: Expensive things feel expensive once. Subscriptions are designed to feel like nothing. The real risk is the cost you never notice.

visual.setup: One visible cost. Or a quiet drip.
visual.question: DO YOU KNOW WHAT YOU PAY EACH MONTH?

packaging.youtubeTitle: Why a Big One-Time Purchase Can Be Cheaper Than Your Subscriptions
packaging.instagramCaption: People estimate they spend 86 dollars a month on subscriptions. The itemized reality averages 219 — over 2.5x more. Big price tags hurt once; recurring charges are designed not to hurt at all. Sources: C+R Research; DellaVigna & Malmendier, American Economic Review. #money #subscriptions #personalfinance #economics
packaging.tiktokCaption: You guess 86 dollars. You actually pay 219. Subscriptions are built to be forgotten. #money #subscriptions #personalfinance
